draft.introduction: Mencius 7A:15 argues that the deepest form of self-knowledge leads directly to knowledge of Heaven. To fully develop one's nature is to serve the cosmic order.

translation.text: Mencius said: "For a person to give full realization to their heart-mind is for them to understand their own nature. To understand one's own nature is to understand Heaven. To preserve one's heart-mind and nourish one's nature is the way to serve Heaven. Whether one dies young or lives to old age makes no difference: one cultivates oneself and awaits whatever comes. This is the way to stand firm in one's destiny."
translation.original_text: 尽其心者，知其性也。知其性，则知天矣

translator_notes.note: The chain of reasoning—heart-mind to nature to Heaven—establishes the Confucian version of theosis: self-cultivation leads to cosmic participation. The Neo-Confucians, especially Wang Yangming, built their entire philosophy on this passage, arguing that the fully realized mind is identical with the principle (li) that governs the universe.
